examples: It must not be thought that any one could have mistaken her for a nervous, sensitive, high-strung nature, cast unduly upon a cold, calculating, and unpoetic world.
Neither sees anything unpoetic about conjuring the smell of cherries from beta-ionone, heliotropine and benzaldehyde.
Dry foods with unpoetic names like spelt, teff and millet have long inhabited the bulk bins at health-food stores.
Barr still speaks in unpoetic terms like "cost per impact" and bankrolls projects like a syndicated poetry series the foundation gives to small-town newspapers to help fill pages.
